title: stenochlaena aculeata
intro: Stenochlaena aculeata, also known as the Australian Stinging Fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Guinea. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparations of Stenochlaena aculeata often involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used as a topical treatment for skin irritations.
